hypertension.tip: Request sauce on the side. Skip chilli sauce. Ask for less gravy.

hyperlipidaemia.tip: Choose steamed over roasted. Limit skin intake.

diabetes.tip: Ask for less rice. Add extra cucumber for fibre.

hypertension.tip: Coconut rice + ikan bilis is high sodium. Skip the fried anchovies.

hyperlipidaemia.tip: Coconut milk is high in saturated fat. Skip fried chicken; pick the cucumber.

diabetes.tip: Coconut rice spikes blood sugar. Halve the rice, add veg.

hypertension.tip: Broth is very high sodium. Ask for soup separate, drink less of it.

hyperlipidaemia.tip: Low fat. Pick soup over dry (no lard/sauce).

diabetes.tip: Choose kway teow or more fishballs, less noodles.

hypertension.tip: Dark sauce + lard = very high sodium. Share a plate.

hyperlipidaemia.tip: Fried in lard. Ask for less oil, skip the cockles' fat.

diabetes.tip: High GI flat noodles + sweet sauce. Limit portion.

hypertension.tip: Ask for no salt added and skip the evaporated milk.

hyperlipidaemia.tip: Lean fish, low fat. Skip the fried fish version.

diabetes.tip: High protein, low GI. A solid everyday choice.

hyperlipidaemia.tip: Condensed milk adds saturated fat. Ask for less milk.

diabetes.tip: Very high sugar. Order 'teh tarik siu dai' (less sweet) or kosong.
Ask for no syrup or 'kosong' to keep sugar low.

hypertension.tip: The dark sauce base and pickled green chillies are packed with sodium. Ask for less sauce.

hyperlipidaemia.tip: Contains lard oil in the seasoning. Request for 'no lard' (mai po) and skip fried wantons.

diabetes.tip: Refined egg noodles spike glucose. Avoid eating the entire noodle portion and focus on the leafy greens and protein.

name: Economy Rice (Cai Fan - 2 Veg + 1 Lean Protein)

hypertension.tip: Avoid dishes drenched in rich gravies, curries, or braised sauces to cut sodium.

hyperlipidaemia.tip: Follow the 1/4 protein, 1/2 veggies rule. Select steamed egg, tofu, or steamed fish over deep-fried variants.

diabetes.tip: Ask for less rice or brown rice if available. Ensure half the plate is filled with non-fried leafy vegetables.

hypertension.tip: Naturally low in sodium. Avoid adding extra salty preserved radish (chai poh).

hyperlipidaemia.tip: Highly plant-based with healthy fats from peanuts and sesame seeds. Exceptional choice.

diabetes.tip: Excellent low-GI option due to high vegetable and tofu content. Pair with brown rice for a flat glycemic response.

name: Yong Tau Foo (Clear Soup - 6 Items, No Noodles)

hypertension.tip: Clear soup is high in sodium. Avoid drinking the broth entirely and skip sweet/chilli dip.

hyperlipidaemia.tip: Choose fresh greens, tofu, seaweed, and egg. Avoid deep-fried items and fishcakes containing hidden fats.

diabetes.tip: A fantastic low-carb, nutrient-dense choice when ordered without noodles or heavy starches.

hypertension.tip: The herbal/beef stock is very high in sodium. Drink minimal broth.

hyperlipidaemia.tip: Request lean beef slices instead of beef brisket, tendon, or beef balls which contain higher saturated fats.

diabetes.tip: Thick bee hoon or kway teow has a moderate-to-high glycemic index. Limit noodle consumption.

hypertension.tip: Ask to omit the crispy fried pork lard bits and limit the savory sweet paste.

hyperlipidaemia.tip: Low in saturated fats as it relies heavily on stewed turnip, bean sprouts, and egg. Omit lard bits.

diabetes.tip: A good high-fiber snack choice. Ask the operator for less sweet sauce (manis) to minimize simple sugars.

hypertension.tip: The anchovy broth and ikan bilis toppings are heavily salted. Minimize soup intake.

hyperlipidaemia.tip: The base soup is lean, but ask for fewer fried anchovies and ensure no lard oil is added to the bowl.

diabetes.tip: Fresh wheat noodles are carbohydrate-dense. Request extra green leafy vegetables (mani cai) to balance the glycemic impact.

hypertension.tip: Spicy spiced broth contains massive sodium counts. Do not drink the soup.

hyperlipidaemia.tip: Uses shredded chicken breast which is lean. Avoid adding any deep-fried potato patties (bergedel).

diabetes.tip: Yellow noodles have high starch values. Ask to substitute with yellow noodles or reduce noodle volume.

hypertension.tip: Extremely low sodium, safe hydration alternative.

hyperlipidaemia.tip: Contains zero saturated fats or cholesterol. Whole grain residue contains soluble fibers.

diabetes.tip: Request 'Siu Dai' (less sweet) or homemade variants with no added sugar. Avoid commercial pre-packaged syrups.
